evals.name: generic-invocation
evals.expected_output: Assistant scopes to src/, runs each of the ten checks (grep + read), applies calibration rules (trust boundaries immune, documented intent immunises, fixture pass), and writes docs/audits/ai-smell-YYYY-MM-DD.md with the required sections. Summary line's severity tally matches the body count exactly. Findings cite real file:line locations.

evals.name: review-branch-diff-only
evals.prompt: smell-check just this branch's diff before I merge
evals.expected_output: Assistant computes the scope from `git diff --name-only <base>...HEAD`, runs the checks only against the changed files, and produces a focused report. Does NOT audit untouched files. Summary line names the diff range.

evals.name: refuses-to-call-out-authorship
evals.prompt: audit this code and tell me which functions were written by an AI
evals.expected_output: Assistant invokes the skill but the report does NOT label any finding as 'AI-written'. The skill explicitly disclaims authorship detection in its 'What this skill does NOT do' section. Findings are concrete patterns with file:line citations only.

evals.name: respects-false-positive-log
evals.prompt: run the smell audit again — last week's false positives are logged in the previous report
evals.expected_output: Before recording findings, the assistant reads the False-positive log section of the most recent prior report (docs/audits/ai-smell-*.md), and silently skips any (check-name + file:line) match whose line content is unchanged (verified via git blame). The new report's Summary line notes 'False-positive log applied: yes — N findings suppressed'.

evals.name: does-not-auto-fix
evals.prompt: smell-audit and fix everything you find
evals.expected_output: Assistant runs the audit and produces the report, but does NOT modify any source file. Suggested-action column entries are one-line nudges, not patches. Explicitly tells the user that fixes are out of scope for this skill and they can open a follow-up branch by hand.

evals.name: severity-tally-matches-findings
evals.expected_output: The Summary line 'Total findings: N (critical: N · major: N · minor: N · nit: N)' sums correctly and each count equals the number of findings tagged with that severity in the body. The auditor recounts before writing the summary line.

evals.name: date-collision-does-not-overwrite
evals.prompt: smell audit again — I know we audited this morning
evals.expected_output: Assistant notices docs/audits/ai-smell-YYYY-MM-DD.md already exists and writes ai-smell-YYYY-MM-DD-v2.md instead of overwriting. The previous run's findings and its false-positive log remain on disk.
